name: Buryatia (Russia)

colors.symbolism: the eternal blue sky and Lake Baikal; the traditional sacred colour of the Mongol-Buryat peoples
moral purity, hospitality, and the good thoughts of the Buryat people
Buddhism, the predominant religion of the Buryats

description: A horizontal triband of blue (broadest), white and yellow, with the golden soyombo (a stylised flame above a sun and crescent moon) in the upper hoist of the blue stripe.
history: Adopted on 29 October 1992 by the Supreme Soviet of the Republic of Buryatia. The soyombo emblem and the colour palette draw on traditional Mongol-Buryat Buddhist symbolism.

features.symbolism: blue stands for the eternal blue sky and the waters of Lake Baikal; white represents the moral purity, hospitality and good intentions of the Buryat people; yellow represents Buddhism, the dominant faith of the Buryats.
the soyombo, a traditional Mongol-Buryat ideogram showing a crescent moon, the sun, and a flame; symbolises eternal life, the heavens, and the hearth (family).
